title: actinodaphne malabarica
intro: Actinodaphne malabarica, also known as Malabar boxwood, is a plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae, commonly found in the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Actinodaphne malabarica include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. Actinodaphne malabarica is often used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to make teas and tonics that can be used to alleviate various health problems.
